what_it_does: Defines an HTML presentation specimen that builds a decision-card mockup from an inline decision record, derives slide content from that record, renders both through the DNA runtime, and mounts the explained view into the page.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
Loads shared piece and phone styles from /surface/styles.
Loads /surface/runtime/archetype.js before running the inline async script.
Defines one pending binary decision record at address decision://global/merge-sa-authorize.
Fetches /dna/layouts.json and uses the decision-card archetype from it.
Renders a portrait decision card with address and resolver values for explanation_source and state.
Builds slide content that explains the decision surface as system-drawn, live-explained, decided in place, and responsive.
Appends the rendered explained view into #mount.
standouts: The page separates the generated card specimen from the slide explanation that frames it.
The decision record supplies both the mockup content and the slide anchors.
The resolver injects explanatory text and pending state at render time.
conventions: Uses a minimal HTML shell with #mount as the render target.
Keeps the specimen data inline inside the page script.
Uses an async IIFE for fetch-render-mount flow.
Relies on shared runtime and style assets by absolute site paths.
Uses record.identity as the source object for human-facing slide fields.
concerns: No visible error handling for failed layout fetches, missing archetypes, or missing DNA runtime.
Inline data, explanatory copy, and render orchestration are coupled in one file.
The page depends on network font loading for its intended typography.
notes: Brief this file as a self-contained presentation specimen for a system-rendered decision card plus explanatory slide content.
questions: What shape does DNA.renderExplained expect for the slide object?
Is the explanation_source resolver text meant to remain inline here or come from another memory source at runtime?
Should the orientation always be portrait for this specimen?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches: it presents a decision card mockup alongside slide content and shows both as generated from a decision record and archetype.
apparent_themes: decision record as source of truth
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate how a decision surface can be drawn from structured decision data while also generating the surrounding explanation for a presentation context.
novelty: Combines a rendered UI specimen and its explanatory slide frame from the same inline decision record, using the renderer as part of the presentation itself.
contribution: Contributes a concrete decision-card presentation fixture: structured decision data, option semantics, generated explanatory slide copy, and the render path that joins an archetype-rendered card with an explained view.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML specimen that renders a pending binary decision card and derived explanatory slide content through the DNA archetype runtime.
intention_for_embedding: Created to show a system-drawn decision surface that explains itself and supports in-place reversible choice.
